title: Oracle For Separating Local Edge-Mode Proof From Cited-Source Fusion Gap
summary: Detailed oracle for the Lecture Three honesty question about local continuum proof coverage versus the narrower residual enrichment route and scope boundaries.
assumptions: The answer should separate local coverage, the residual enrichment route, and the scope conflict cleanly.
scope: Detailed answer key for the Lecture Three proof-versus-fusion honesty question.

source_anchors.notes: The original source is present through a dedicated theorem, while denser archival mapping remains follow-up work.

derivation_spine: State that the local Witten-side continuum proof obligations are explicit.
State that the Jackiw-Rebbi, anomaly-inflow, and Callan-Harvey cancellation ingredients are already source-grade locally.
State that the remaining follow-up is denser archival mapping rather than a missing core theorem.
State that the continuum parity-anomaly story is not the same thing as the lattice no-go theorem.
mandatory_logical_moves: Separate local continuum proof completion from the residual enrichment route.
Name the actual gap and follow-up task.
common_failure_patterns: Claiming the cited-source route is still missing a core Jackiw-Rebbi or anomaly-inflow theorem.
Ignoring the explicit Callan-Harvey cancellation fragment when describing what is already local.
Collapsing the continuum edge-mode family into the lattice no-go theorem.
Failing to identify any current local proof coverage.
grading_rubric: Pass requires all three distinctions: local source-grade coverage, the residual enrichment route, and the scope boundary.
failure_triggers: Fail if the answer says the Jackiw-Rebbi / Callan-Harvey route is already exhaustively complete when the designated enrichment task still remains.
Fail if the answer identifies the continuum story with the lattice theorem.
